summary: Verified bug fix for UPI-ID copy button on PayManualQR page. The primary bug (unhandled promise rejection / error toast on clipboard.writeText failure) is FULLY FIXED. All user-facing assertions from the review request pass: success toast appears, clipboard actually contains the UPI value, no error toast, no DOMException/NotAllowedError, no unhandled rejection — even under 10 rapid clicks. Regressions on pay-done-btn -> pay-form-card and /app/home / banner-home also pass.
critical_code_review_comments: copy() helper in PayManualQR.jsx is now correctly async-awaiting navigator.clipboard.writeText, guards with window.isSecureContext, and has a real legacyCopy fallback returning a boolean — matches the intended fix described in the review request. No further changes needed here.

Minor suggestion (not blocking): the catch block re-runs legacyCopy(text) which was already attempted in the try branch when the secure-context clipboard write throws; this is intentional (secure-context failed → try execCommand once), but slightly opaque. A short inline comment would help future maintainers.
test_credentials: User: 7802655202 / Passw0rd! (RW001798). Admin: 9999999999 / Admin@12345. Program used for pay page test: 2712540e-5d15-46f8-90bb-7b9966f883c6 (TEST_Program A, one-time, no active purchase). UPI in seeded payment_settings: riyor97236576@barodampay.

Used existing payment_settings row (upi_id=riyor97236576@barodampay) — the review request's expected 'riyorawellness@hdfc' UPI was not present in DB; the actual seeded value was used and the test still validates the exact copy semantics (clipboard content == visible UPI value).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Playwright browser context in this preview environment DOES support grant_permissions(['clipboard-read','clipboard-write']) and navigator.clipboard.readText() works, so the primary bug can be fully validated end-to-end. The /admin/qa/brv regression check was NOT performed because after admin login and navigation to /admin/qa/brv the page rendered the public marketing landing (session/token persistence issue on cross-navigation in isolated Playwright context — NOT a product bug and NOT related to this frontend change). If BRV suite validation is required, it should be run through the admin dashboard by navigating within the SPA (link click) rather than a fresh page.goto.

primary_bug_fix.rapid-10-clicks-no-error-queue: PASS (0 error toasts, 0 rejections after 10 rapid clicks, button remains visible+enabled)

regressions.admin-brv-36-of-36: NOT TESTED (see context note above — unrelated to this fix)
